name: monaco-editor
visual_summary: A Monaco editor component to render a code editor in HTML. It's used when a developer wants to give users ability to edit code. It can be used in a variety of applications, including coding tutorials, documentation, and configuration interfaces.
generation_prompt:
Create a production-ready button component with the following characteristics:

Name: monaco-editor
Description: A Monaco editor component to render a code editor in HTML. It's used when a developer wants to give users ability to edit code. It can be used in a variety of applications, including coding tutorials, documentation, and configuration interfaces.
Style: default, vs-dark
Interactions: none, clickable
Tags: editor, code_editor, html

Requirements:
- Works in React with TypeScript by default but adaptable to Vue, Svelte, or plain HTML/CSS
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Fully accessible with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
- Responsive and mobile-friendly
- No external dependencies beyond React and Tailwind unless necessary
- Include all necessary imports
- Export as default with sensible prop types

Generate clean production-ready code only. No explanations.
Reference implementation (adapt don't copy):
// elements/monaco-editor.html
<div x-data="{
        monacoContent: '',
        monacoLanguage: 'html',
        monacoPlaceholder: true,
        monacoPlaceholderText: 'Start typing here',
        monacoLoader: true,
        monacoFontSize: '15px',
        monacoId: $id('monaco-editor'),
        monacoEditor(editor){
            editor.onDidChangeModelContent((e) => {
                this.monacoContent = editor.getValue();
                this.updatePlaceholder(editor.getValue());
            }
